value.session_context: Friday close for the US cash-equity week; AP reported final index closes and weekly changes.

text: Friday, June 26, 2026 was a regular US market session for the cash-equity analysis basis used here. AP published final Friday closes for the S&P 500, Dow, Nasdaq Composite, and Russell 2000, and reported weekly performance through the close.

calculation.method: AP close summary: S&P 500 7,354.02, -3.47, less than -0.1%; Dow 51,876.11, -44.51, -0.1%; Nasdaq 25,297.62, -60.99, -0.2%; Russell 2000 3,010.08, +2.23, +0.1%. Weekly and year-to-date percentages are AP-reported.

text: US equity indexes closed mixed to slightly lower on Friday, June 26, 2026. The S&P 500 slipped 3.47 points, or less than 0.1%, to 7,354.02; the Dow fell 44.51 points, or 0.1%, to 51,876.11; the Nasdaq Composite lost 60.99 points, or 0.2%, to 25,297.62; and the Russell 2000 rose 2.23 points, or 0.1%, to 3,010.08. For the week, the S&P 500 fell 2.0%, the Nasdaq fell 4.6%, the Dow rose 0.6%, and the Russell 2000 gained 1.0%.

value.sp_500_breadth: nearly two out of every three stocks within the S&P 500 rose
value.interpretation: Positive breadth was offset by high-index-weight AI and technology declines.

text: Market breadth was better than the cap-weighted S&P 500 close implied: AP reported that nearly two out of every three S&P 500 stocks rose, even as the index slipped less than 0.1%. The divergence shows that losses in large AI-linked stocks outweighed broader gains elsewhere.

value.driver: AI valuation and funding concerns, including a New York Times report that OpenAI was considering delaying its IPO to next year.

market_session: US regular cash-equity close and Asia Friday sessions, 2026-06-26
text: AI-linked and chip shares were the main drag. AP identified Micron Technology's 6.7% drop as the heaviest weight on the market and reported ON Semiconductor down 23.7% after agreeing to buy Synaptics in an all-stock deal valued near $7 billion. AP also reported that SpaceX recovered from a 2.9% intraday drop to finish up 0.2%, while Asia's AI-linked losses included SoftBank down 12.5%, SK Hynix down 8.4%, and Samsung Electronics down 5.3%.

brent_crude.basis: AP market wrap; price described as lower than before the US and Israel attacked Iran

ten_year_treasury_yield.basis: AP market wrap, late Thursday comparison

consumer_inflation_expectations.basis: AP cited consumer inflation expectations report

market_session: US regular session close and commodity/rates late-session basis, Friday 2026-06-26
text: Oil and rates eased into the Friday close. AP reported Brent crude down 3.8% to $72.60, below its level before the US and Israel attacked Iran, and the 10-year Treasury yield down to 4.37% from 4.40% late Thursday. AP also reported one-year US consumer inflation expectations edged down to 4.6% from 4.8% in May.

The session showed defensive and fuel-sensitive support underneath the headline weakness. AP reported Eli Lilly up 7.1% after an EMA committee recommendation and American Airlines up 1.7% as oil fell. CNBC reported health care was heading for a weekly gain of more than 7%, consumer staples rose more than 1% on the day, financials gained 0.8%, utilities gained 0.4%, and the S&P 500 information technology sector fell 1%.

value.kashkari_2026_rate_view: one interest rate hike by year-end
value.prior_view: one rate cut by year-end penciled in March
value.context: Kashkari linked the shift to Middle East supply risks, tariffs, fertilizer and energy disruptions, and data-center investment pressure.

text: Fed policy risk stayed live after Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari said he had changed his outlook from one rate cut by year-end to one rate hike by year-end. CNBC reported that May headline PCE inflation rose to 4.1%, the highest since April 2023, while core PCE reached 3.4%, and that inflation has remained above the Fed's 2% goal for five years.
